description:
lib.rs:221 — data_size (a usize from nv_public.data_size()) is cast to u16 with no comment:

    ctx.nv_read(NvAuth::Owner, nv_index_handle, data_size as u16, 0)

This cast is safe in practice because TPM 2.0 NV indices max out at ~2048 bytes, well within u16 range. But a reader unfamiliar with TPM limits must verify this externally. A one-line comment (or a debug_assert / checked cast) would make the safety argument self-contained.

Suggested fix: add a comment or use u16::try_from(data_size).expect("TPM NV index size fits in u16") to make the invariant explicit.
title: data_size as u16 cast is silently correct but undocumented
